lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, are you okay? What happend to you?

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Ugh... Well met, adventurer. I wouldn't go down there if I were you. That trap is an absolute nightmare! The one who created it must be a sadist. I don't know of anyone who has cleared it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh? I've cleared it.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Oh? Are you pulling my leg, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fairly certain that if I did that, it would fall off!

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: No need to add salt in the wound.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Although I am curious, how did you beat the trap?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just made sure to prioritise looking out for the arrows. The fire was fairly easy to get through if I just made sure to take one step at a time.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: I see, you're clearly more skillful than I. I'm not sure if I'd be agile enough to do that. Do tell, what was on the next floor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The place looked magnificent, gold lion statues, coffins untouched by anyone else and plenty of Hallowed marks compared to these floors. Although, the traps were significantly harder than the one below.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: I see. Well, I guess I'd better brush up on my skills if I want to see it for myself. Nothing worth having in life comes easy right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very true, I wish you the best of luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No one has cleared it? How hard can it be?

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Well, unlike the rest of the traps I've seen so far, you have to deal with both an arrow trap and a fire trap at the same time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that does sound hard. Do you have any tips for me?

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Well, individually they aren't that hard to do. The fires are always swapping between on and off. I think that if you run to where the fire just was you should be safe.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: The hardest part for me is the arrows. You have to look forward to watch the fire trap whilst also looking over your shoulder to see where the arrows are.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: I certainly wish there was a way to see both at once. Perhaps it would help if a fellow adventurer was looking down from above to call out where the arrows are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... I'd imagine having an aerial view of the situation would be pretty helpful.

lines.speaker: fishrunner82
lines.text: Of course, but we both know that's impossible for one person! Ouch! Sorry, it hurts to laugh. I wish you luck adventurer!
